pattern: Aggregate per-player color frequencies and check threshold conditions with straightforward counting.
hints: Group the picks by player, then for each player count how many balls of each color they picked.
Player i wins if their maximum single-color count exceeds i (strictly greater than i).
A nested dictionary or 2D array indexed by player and color keeps the counting simple.

edge_inputs.description: Player 0 always wins with a single pick
No player wins: each player picks different colors
Only player 2 wins by having 3 of same color
Max n with all picks for same player
Player 0 is the only one who needs just 1 ball
Player needs i+1 not just i balls

stress.description: Random picks for random players and colors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 10)
    pick_count = rng.randint(1, size)
    pick = [[rng.randint(0, n-1), rng.randint(0, 10)] for _ in range(pick_count)]
    return [n, pick]
